thesis: A warm coffee brand whose signature Baskerville serif and real photography were boxed into flat color bands and thumbnail cards. Three brand-faithful directions, one URL.

variants.label: A · Faithful
variants.thesis: Tomorrow's version of the site you have today — six fixes on the same IA: the broken Google-Maps band becomes a real store-locator card, Baskerville rises from 36px to a 56px hero, categories get real presence, photography is restored.

variants.label: B · Serif
variants.thesis: What if Baskerville stopped whispering in eyebrows and became the structural voice? A ~96px type-spine hero and a numbered editorial index for the product categories.

variants.label: C · Cinematic
variants.thesis: What if motion was part of the identity? Editorial register — full-bleed coffee photography as protagonist, revealed at reading pace with soft parallax and long crossfades.

thesis: A cross-border French agglomération that buried residents' daily tasks under an institutional org-chart, colored tab bars, and tri-color separators. Three brand-faithful directions on the trois-frontières identity.

variants.label: A · Faithful
variants.thesis: Tomorrow's version of the site you have today — a quiet sticky header replaces the colored tab bar, a compact quick-task band and meaningful access cards replace the utility clutter, the territory photo is promoted to a legible full-bleed hero, and one accent rule per section replaces the marquee and tri-color separators.

variants.label: B · Tasks
variants.thesis: What if the homepage led with what residents actually came to do, instead of the org chart of the institution? A task-first « Je cherche… » action grid leads the page; institutional categories retreat to a utility row. IA reprioritised, content unchanged.

variants.label: C · Cinematic
variants.thesis: What if the territory — and the three-link « trois pays » mark that stands for it — became the site's kinetic identity? The full-bleed bridge hero parallaxes up, a rising content plate covers it, and the three-link mark recurs as scroll-revealed dividers (arrival register).

thesis: The world's only binational airport hid its live operational state under a terminal photo and a mega-nav. Three brand-faithful directions foregrounding what travelers actually need.

variants.label: A · Faithful
variants.thesis: Tomorrow's version of the site you have today — an operational hero (flight search plus an at-a-glance strip of next departures, parking availability FR/CH, and security wait) leads the first viewport; corporate and secondary paths recede into a quiet utility tier, and live numerals get a large tabular treatment.

variants.label: B · Binational
variants.thesis: What if « the world's only binational airport » were the layout itself? A France ↔ Suisse structural spine organizes parking, currency (EUR | CHF), road access and wayfinding around a unifying EuroAirport centerline — one airport, two countries, made legible.

variants.label: C · Cinematic
variants.thesis: What if the airport's real-time state WAS the homepage? A departures ticker, count-up on wait/parking/board numerals, capacity bars filling per sector, and live pulse dots — the data is the choreography, headings stay still (live-systems register).
